claim: The operator shall confine all file mutations to its launch working directory, never modifying anything outside it -- including the host harness and the global configuration -- unless the user explicitly requests otherwise.
why: Resolver verdict 2026-07-05, verbatim: 'cah вообще нельзя трогать - он ведь обновится всё сотрет. И он в праве быть собой, без наших вмешательств' + 'нужно создать правило, что работаем строго в папке запуска, пока пользователь не попросит иного'. Trigger: the operator proposed setup_context_hook.py --patch-global --apply, which surgically patched the global host configuration to piggyback a context-cache -- a double fault: (1) it violates host sovereignty (the harness is a guest environment we inhabit, not own), and (2) it is futile, since a host update overwrites the patch anyway. The framework is a guest in the host's home: it lives strictly within the repository it was launched in; everything outside is sovereign and untouched absent an explicit user request. In the Go port the codebase is internal/ + cmd/hotam/ (compiled Go, not interpreted Python); the tools that motivated the original incident (setup_context_hook.py, setup_hooks.py, _claude_settings.py -- all of which wrote into a consumer repo's .claude/ or, in the incident, the global config) are Declared (not ported) in methodology.Tools, so the committed-code write vector that actually fired does not exist in the Go CLI today. The historical ENFORCED mechanism (tests/test_launch_dir_write_scope.py) AST-scanned every committed framework .py for a home-rooted path reference (Path.home() / os.path.expanduser('~...') / a literal starting with '~/') co-located with a filesystem-write sink (open(...,'w'/'a'/'x'), write_text/write_bytes/mkdir/touch/rmtree/unlink/replace/rename/symlink_to/chmod/makedirs/remove/copy*/move) and failed RED on any hit -- that scanner was NOT ported to Go (no equivalent Go test AST-scans internal/ + cmd/hotam/ for os.UserHomeDir / home-path writes). FOUND ISSUE (not fixed): this requirement was ENFORCED in the Python era via the AST scanner; in the Go port the scanner was never ported and the enforcement was downgraded to PROSE. The rule's residual half (a live agent shelling out through bash at runtime to touch the home dir) always was and remains discipline-of-prose (R-agent-conduct-is-rules-not-tests). The enforcement/enforced_by are out of scope for this content batch; documented as debt rather than reworded into a lie. The original WHY stands: the framework works strictly within its launch directory until the user asks otherwise.
